data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "A prolonged drought from c.1220–1160 BCE led to successive crop failures in the eastern Mediterranean, undermining the agricultural base of Mycenaean Greece, Hittite Anatolia, Ugarit and Levantine city‑states.", "mechanism": "environmental stress (climate‑induced water scarcity)", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city‑states"], "excludes": ["Egypt"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Pollen cores from the Levant and Anatolia show no significant decline in moisture levels during 1200–1150 BCE", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Chronological narrative links the onset of aridity with documented famines and decline in state capacities. Scholarly register, cites recent paleoclimatic data."}
